allowedShipGuardActions: Improve ShipGuard report fields, Markdown, rules, docs, plugin guidance, or public fixtures.
Use private app evidence only to choose the shape of synthetic public eval coverage.
forbiddenActions: Do not edit the scanned app from this report.
Do not open target-app redesign, icon, haptic, motion, or localization tasks from this report.

coherenceRisks.evidence: Synthetic learning source signals choose education while the report must avoid utility-only design advice.
coherenceRisks.title: Education guidance must be app-type tailored

proofBoundary.expectedArtifact: ios-report-quality.json plus fixture coverage for design app-type tailoring.
proofBoundary.localProof: Run shipguard ios report-quality on this synthetic design fixture.
proofBoundary.manualProof: A human may later authorize target-app design work, but this fixture does not authorize it.

designCoherenceBoundary.purpose: Keep design-system coherence findings as ShipGuard product-QA evidence until target-app work is separately authorized.

shipguardNextAction.expectedArtifact: A public synthetic report-quality fixture or rule update that checks app-type tailoring without private app data.
shipguardNextAction.failureMeaning: Private app design evidence can still become unreviewed app remediation advice instead of ShipGuard product QA.
shipguardNextAction.sourceQuestion: Did the report tailor advice to the app type instead of applying one universal design rule?
shipguardNextAction.successCondition: Report-quality fails if a design report omits app-type tailoring or hides target-app authorization boundaries.

copyTone.stance: specific to the app task and audience

motion.reason: Motion should clarify learning state, feedback, and recovery rather than apply utility-only restraint.

nextAction.expectedArtifact: A same-flow screenshot or preview receipt plus one note mapping the learning-progress profile to source signals.
nextAction.failureMeaning: The design report remains an inventory, not an app-type-specific design QA recommendation.
nextAction.manualProof: Review one synthetic learning flow and confirm motion, haptics, visual density, and copy guidance match the education profile rather than a universal design checklist.
nextAction.successCondition: The report explains why learning-progress is the right profile for education and avoids utility-only advice.

designTailoring.risk: Generic utility restraint can make learning feedback feel flat, while generic game delight can distract from comprehension.

findings.evidence: Synthetic learning source signals choose education while the report must avoid utility-only design advice.
findings.proof: Review the Design Tailoring Contract and attach one preview or screenshot receipt for the synthetic learning flow.
findings.proofGuidance: Review the Design Tailoring Contract and attach one preview or screenshot receipt for the synthetic learning flow.
findings.recommendation: Use the learning-progress profile for motion, haptics, visual density, and copy guidance.
findings.title: Education guidance must be app-type tailored

fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: Synthetic public fixture. No private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Did the report tailor advice to the app type instead of applying one universal design rule?

checks.question: Can lesson status and primary actions be distinguished without decorative color dependence?
Does the learning flow show lesson goal, current task, and next step in that order?
Do lesson cards, progress labels, and controls share a deliberate grid?
Are related learning controls grouped and recovery actions separated?
Are cards, buttons, and success states reused across the learning flow?
Is progress feedback visually weighted without overwhelming the lesson content?
Does spacing give comprehension room without wasting mobile screen density?
Do color, type, symbols, copy, motion, and haptics feel like one education product?
Does motion clarify progress or recovery while respecting Reduce Motion?
Are haptics milestone-aware, sparse, and left unclaimed until device proof exists?
Are visual claims backed by iPhone preview or Devspace evidence?
Is the guidance tuned for education comprehension instead of generic utility polish?

professionalDesignPrincipleVocabulary.source: ShipGuard native design QA vocabulary inspired by professional visual-design principles.

reportQualityQuestions: Did the report tailor advice to the app type instead of applying one universal design rule?

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard design app-type tailoring report-quality behavior.
